bead.title: agent: type worker provider-process failures before generic failure
bead.description:
PROBLEM

Underspecified worker/provider-process failures are a DDx correctness bug. DDx/FIZ is the abstraction that should keep work moving across provider availability and local configuration variance. If a provider executable is missing, a harness process exits early, model routing fails, or Fizeau returns a pre-dispatch/provider-boundary error, `ddx work` must classify it with a typed provider/process reason, record fallback/stop evidence, clean up any children, and continue when an alternate route is eligible. It must not surface a generic worker failure, raw "routing under-specified" text, or leave the bead/operator unable to tell whether this is provider_auth, provider_model_unavailable, provider_harness_unavailable, provider_connectivity, no_viable_provider, provider_config_invalid, or unknown_provider_failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/agent/provider_failure.go:1-17` defines the intended DDx/Fizeau boundary contract: generic untyped provider failures are product bugs, and each provider failure needs typed reason, retryability, fallback decision, and durable evidence.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:140-142` still returns `fmt.Errorf("agent: execute: %w", err)` for Fizeau `Execute` setup failures before the event stream exists; every caller must prove this error is classified and does not fall through as generic execution/worker failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:337-385` bounds event-stream silence/tool loops, but provider-process startup/early-exit failures can occur before useful stream evidence, so they need a separate typed classification/cleanup path.
- `cli/cmd/zero_config_work_test.go:15-27` documents the prior "routing under-specified" regression and explicitly avoids real provider dispatch; coverage must be extended from "do not print under-specified" to "classify provider-process failure and choose fallback/stop semantics."
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_escalation_aborted_test.go:18-60` and `:285-342` prove provider_connectivity fallback semantics after reports exist, but do not prove raw provider-process failures are converted into those reports before worker/operator surfaces see them.

PROPOSED FIX

Add an end-to-end worker/process-boundary classification path for provider process startup, missing executable, early child exit, model-unavailable, under-specified routing, and Fizeau pre-dispatch errors. Normalize them through the provider failure taxonomy, write `ProviderFailureEvidence`, include requested/resolved harness/provider/model plus retryability and fallback_stop_reason, and ensure unpinned workers automatically retry eligible alternate routes while pinned workers stop loudly with hard_pin_exhausted. Ensure cleanup reaps provider-process children before the worker continues.

NON-SCOPE

Do not move model catalog/routing ownership out of Fizeau. Do not call real Claude/Codex/Gemini providers in tests. Do not silently widen explicit operator pins. Do not weaken existing provider_connectivity/no_viable_provider fallback tests.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestWorkProviderProcessMissingExecutableClassified` uses a fake service or fake provider process startup failure equivalent to `exec.ErrNotFound`; `ddx work --once` records `typed_failure=provider_harness_unavailable`, does not print raw "routing under-specified" or generic "execution_failed", unclaims the bead, and leaves no provider child processes.
2. `TestWorkProviderProcessEarlyExitClassifiedWithEvidence` simulates a provider child that exits before emitting a final event; DDx records structured provider/process evidence including requested_harness, requested_provider, requested_model, typed_failure, retryable, fallback_attempted, fallback_stop_reason when applicable, exit_code/stderr excerpt or equivalent process detail, and cleanup_result.
3. `TestWorkProviderModelUnavailableFallsBackWhenUnpinned` simulates model-not-found/model-unavailable from the first route; an unpinned worker records the failed route and retries an alternate eligible route without operator intervention.
4. `TestWorkProviderFailureHonorsHardPins` pins harness/provider/model, simulates the same provider failure, and proves DDx does not widen the pin; it stops with `fallback_stop_reason=hard_pin_exhausted` and actionable operator output.
5. `TestWorkStatusAndOperatorAttentionSurfaceTypedProviderFailure` proves `ddx work status --json` and operator-attention/evidence surfaces show the typed provider/process failure instead of an underspecified worker failure.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkProviderProcessMissingExecutableClassified|TestWorkProviderProcessEarlyExitClassifiedWithEvidence|TestWorkProviderModelUnavailableFallsBackWhenUnpinned|TestWorkProviderFailureHonorsHardPins|TestWorkStatusAndOperatorAttentionSurfaceTypedProviderFailure'` passes.\n7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
